meta_title: For Students: API integration guide to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to API integration guide and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: API Integration Guide to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about no portfolio. Learn to build high-impact API integrations with Antigravity and Claude Code in this beginner-friendly developer tutorial.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' trap, you must build functional, real-world API integrations. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent implementation, you can transform abstract concepts into live, data-driven applications that demonstrate technical competence to recruiters and prove you can solve complex engineering problems.
content.intro:
The cold reality of the job market is that a degree is just a piece of paper; your portfolio is your currency. Every day you spend without a live project is a day you fall behind the competition. The anxiety of having nothing to show is paralyzing, but the fix is simpler than you think: stop studying theory and start connecting systems.

This guide is your tactical blueprint. We are moving past 'Hello World' to build professional-grade API integrations. Using Antigravity to handle the heavy lifting and Claude Code to accelerate your logic, you will ship a project that doesn't just look good on a resume—it functions like a production-ready application.

steps.title: Define Your API Ecosystem
steps.description: Before writing code, identify a public API (like OpenWeather or CoinGecko) that solves a specific user problem. Clarity here ensures your project has a clear 'Why'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a project structure for a Next.js application that integrates the OpenWeather API. Include a clean folder structure for services and types.

steps.title: Configure Secure Environment Variables
steps.description: Hardcoding keys is the quickest way to fail a technical interview. Learn to manage secrets locally using .env files.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a secure configuration module that loads API keys from .env and throws an error if the key is missing.

steps.title: Implement Robust Data Fetching
steps.description: Don't just fetch; handle errors gracefully. A senior engineer anticipates failure. We will build a service that handles rate-limiting and timeouts.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript service function to fetch weather data with Axios, including a retry mechanism and error handling for 404s.

steps.title: Build a Resilient UI State
steps.description: Bridge the gap between your API data and the user interface. Display loading states, error alerts, and the final data set.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a React component that displays weather data, showing a spinner while loading and an error message if the API call fails.

steps.title: Optimize and Document Your Work
steps.description: Your README is as important as your code. Document how to run the project and explain the technical challenges you overcame.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md for this project that highlights the API integration, error handling, and tech stack.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write-readme --features 'API integration, error handling, TypeScript'

pro_tips: Always wrap external API calls in a dedicated service layer to decouple the UI from the data source.
Use mock data during development to save your API rate limits and avoid unnecessary network costs.
Include a 'Troubleshooting' section in your README; it shows interviewers you can debug complex issues.
Add a 'Future Improvements' section to your project to demonstrate a product-mindset and long-term thinking.
Deploy your project to Vercel or Netlify immediately; a live URL is worth ten times more than a GitHub link.
resources: MDN Web Docs on Fetch API: https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Fetch_API
content.next_steps: Once deployed, add one more API to your project—like a location service—to turn it into a multi-source dashboard. Then, share your live link on LinkedIn with a breakdown of the technical hurdles you cleared.
content.conversion_block: Stop waiting for permission to be a developer. You have the tools, the guide, and the blueprint to build a portfolio that commands attention. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
